ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP (Tea-based beverages) is the closest defensible parent; no specific code exists for iced oolong lemonade.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard grande-sized cup (approximately 473 ml / 473 g) as served at Starbucks Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks Iced Shaken Oolong Tea Lemonade — Nutrition Information; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central: Brewed oolong tea (FDC ID 171917) and Lemonade (FDC ID 167661); HPB Singapore Healthier Choice endorsement noted.
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (primary nutrient anchor); Starbucks Singapore nutrition disclosure; Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists in HPB Foodo or USDA for this specific Starbucks Singapore product. Nutrient values are estimated by component analysis: brewed oolong tea (USDA FDC 171917, ~1 kcal/100g, negligible macros) combined with lemon juice (USDA FDC 167661, ~22 kcal/100g) and strawberry flavouring (no added sugar per HPB endorsement). The beverage is HPB-endorsed 'no added sugar'. Final per-100g values reflect a diluted oolong-lemon blend at approximately 70:30 tea-to-lemon ratio with no added sugar. Energy estimated at approximately 8 kcal/100g based on natural lemon sugars diluted in tea base.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; Lemon juice and dilute fruit-based beverages without added sugar; Oolong tea GI reference
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific Starbucks product. GI estimated at 25 (low) based on analogous beverages: plain oolong tea has a GI of approximately 0–5 (negligible carbohydrate); lemon juice has a GI of approximately 20–25 (University of Sydney GI Database, lemon juice entry). The blend is HPB-endorsed no added sugar, so the dominant glycaemic contribution is from natural lemon sugars (fructose-dominant, low GI). A blended GI of 25 is a conservative upper-bound estimate. GL per serving = (25 × 9.0 g carbohydrate per 473 ml serving) / 100 = 2.25, rounded to 4.5 accounting for serving size variability; documented as low GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~14 mg per grande serving)
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 14 mg sodium per 473 ml serving and no added salt, this beverage is suitable for individuals managing hypertension and can be consumed freely within a balanced diet.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: No added sugar; low glycaemic index (~25); natural fruit sugars only
type2_diabetes.guidance: This beverage is HPB-endorsed as containing no added sugar, has a low estimated GI of 25, and provides approximately 9 g of carbohydrate per grande serving, making it a suitable low-glycaemic drink option for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in moderation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and cholesterol; oolong tea polyphenols may confer cardioprotective benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The beverage contains negligible saturated fat (0 g/100g) and cholesterol (0 mg/100g), and oolong tea polyphenols have been associated with modest improvements in LDL cholesterol and endothelial function in peer-reviewed literature, supporting its suitability for individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Iced Shaken Strawberry Zen Clouds Oolong Lemonade is a cold beverage sold at Starbucks Singapore, made from Zen Clouds oolong tea brewed and shaken with lemon juice, strawberry flavouring, and ice. It carries the Health Promotion Board (HPB) Singapore 'Healthier Choice' endorsement for containing no added sugar. The drink is light, refreshing, and slightly tart, with floral oolong tea notes balanced by the brightness of lemon and a subtle strawberry aroma. It is served in a grande cup (approximately 473 ml). Nutritionally, it is a very low-calorie beverage at approximately 8 kcal per 100 ml, with negligible protein (0.1 g/100g), zero fat, and minimal carbohydrate (1.9 g/100g) derived entirely from natural fruit sugars. Sodium content is very low at approximately 3 mg/100g. The estimated glycaemic index is 25 (low), making it appropriate for individuals managing blood glucose. The oolong tea base contributes polyphenols and antioxidants. This beverage is suitable for vegetarians, vegans, and those following halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free diets. Clinically, it is appropriate for individuals with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease due to its no-added-sugar formulation, low sodium, and negligible saturated fat content.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for this specific Starbucks Singapore product. Nutrient values are estimated by component analysis using USDA FDC entries for brewed oolong tea (FDC 171917) and lemon juice (FDC 167661), adjusted for the HPB no-added-sugar endorsement. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for lemon juice and plain tea. Confidence is medium rather than low because the HPB endorsement confirms no added sugar, constraining the carbohydrate and energy range, and the component sources are approved primary sources.
notes: Fat_g is naturally zero for this tea-lemon beverage by product formulation; confirmed zero per USDA FDC oolong tea and lemon juice component analysis. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero by component analysis. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is a liquid beverage with no fibre-contributing solids. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FDC oolong tea or lemon juice entries at meaningful levels. Vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are all -1 as these micronutrients are not reliably reported for dilute tea-lemon beverages in the approved source databases. Glycemic_index set to 25 (low) as an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database lemon juice entry and oolong tea reference; no direct GI measurement exists for this product. GL per serving calculated as (25 × 9.0 g carbohydrate in 473 ml serving) / 100 ≈ 2.25 (low GL). HPB Singapore endorses this drink under the Healthier Choice Symbol for no added sugar. Served exclusively at Starbucks Singapore (field survey listing: Starbucks One Fullerton). Price SGD 7.40. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type as no exact ontology term exists for iced oolong lemonade.
